name: Priya Patel

ib_courses.course: Extended Essay (Psychology — on social media and adolescent identity formation)

academic.note: Priya completed the full IB Diploma Programme with a predicted score of 40/45 (IB scores range from 24-45; 40+ places her in the top ~3% globally). Her 3.78 UW GPA reflects a genuinely rigorous program — a 3.78 at a competitive private school on the full IB Diploma is not equivalent to a 3.78 at a less demanding school.

sat.note: First attempt: 1410. Second: 1460. Third: 1490. Consistent incremental improvement. Not a standout test taker but well above most school medians.

activities.name: Model United Nations
activities.role: Secretary-General (senior year), Head Chair (junior year), Delegate (prior years)
activities.description: Led Princeton Day School's MUN team. As Secretary-General, organized a 400-student conference hosted at her school. Represented PDS at Harvard HMUN, UPenn PMUNC, and Georgetown NAIMUN conferences. Placed Best Delegate at PMUNC junior year.
awards: Secretary-General — PDS Model UN Conference (organized for 400+ students)

activities.name: Varsity Field Hockey
activities.description: 4-year varsity player at a competitive private school program. Team reached state semifinals junior year. Not at a recruited athlete level but a committed and contributing player.

activities.name: Psychology Research Independent Study
activities.description: Conducted independent research on adolescent social media use and identity formation under supervision of a Princeton University graduate student (family connection). Research became her IB Extended Essay. Submitted findings to the NJ Science Olympiad research competition; received honorable mention.
awards: NJ Science Olympiad Research: Honorable Mention

activities.name: School Newspaper — 'The Panther'
activities.description: Writes and edits the school's monthly feature pieces. Notable piece: investigative article on mental health counseling wait times at NJ public schools, cited by a local NJ newspaper.

activities.name: Habitat for Humanity
activities.role: Build Site Volunteer, Campus Chapter Co-Leader
activities.description: Participates in monthly build days. Co-leads school chapter logistics. Has completed 6 weekend builds total.

activities.name: Bharatanatyam (Classical Indian Dance)
activities.description: Trained since age 3. Completed arangetram (debut solo recital) at age 16. Performs at cultural events and festivals in the NJ/NY area. Has taught beginner students at her dance school for the past two years.
awards: NJ Indian Cultural Association: Featured Performer (2023, 2024)

intended_career: Clinical psychology or public policy, with interest in adolescent mental health systems

essays.main_essay_theme: Explores the tension between her bharatanatyam training — where every gesture is codified by 2000 years of tradition — and her MUN work, where improvisation and persuasion are everything. Uses this to reflect on when rules serve us and when they constrain us. Sophisticated framing, well-executed.
essays.supplemental_notes: Why Major essays are substantive and connect to her IB Extended Essay research. Why School essays are well-researched and specific. One essay about a MUN moment is particularly compelling. School context letter from college counselor explains IB rigor clearly.
essays.writing_style: Polished, intellectually confident. Occasionally academic in tone — reads as someone who has had writing coaching. Competent but less distinctive voice compared to students with more unusual backgrounds.

hooks: South Asian / Indian American — group that is neither URM nor overrepresented uniformly (school-dependent)
Full IB Diploma with predicted 40/45 — strong signal of academic preparation
Private school — admission context is important for GPA interpretation
Geographic: New Jersey is a moderately competitive state
additional_info: Father is a physician, mother is a software engineer at a pharmaceutical company. Family can contribute $70,000+/year. Not eligible for need-based aid at most schools. Will apply to some schools without expecting aid. Parents have strong preferences for name-brand universities.
special_circumstances: No significant hardships or unusual circumstances. Profile is defined by consistent achievement in a rigorous environment rather than a compelling personal narrative of overcoming difficulty. Admissions context: at highly selective schools, the absence of a standout spike or unusual story may be the limiting factor, not the academic profile.

high_school.name: Princeton Day School
high_school.challenge_index_note: IB-based program; Challenge Index (AP metric) not applicable
high_school.committee_context: Elite NJ independent school with ~$47,000/year tuition and a full IB Diploma program. Grading is rigorous — a 3.78 UW on the IB Diploma is not equivalent to a 3.78 at a less demanding school. The school sends a detailed school profile and college counselor context letter that admissions officers at selective schools know and trust. 38% of graduates attend highly selective institutions annually.
high_school.gpa_adjustment_note: 3.78 UW on a full IB Diploma at a rigorously graded elite private school reads as approximately a 4.0+ in a conventional context. The IB predicted score of 40/45 (global top 3%) is the more reliable signal of academic ceiling.
